name: Phantom
downloadable: none (releases code and policies, not annotations)
suitability: descriptive-only: a code-only human-to-robot pipeline that converts human video into robot-compatible pairs, no clusterable language corpus
focus_en: Trains robot manipulation from human-only video by converting each demonstration into robot-compatible observation-action pairs via hand-pose estimation and visual editing.
focus_cn: 仅用人类视频训练机器人操作：通过手部姿态估计与视觉编辑，将每段演示转换为与机器人兼容的观测-动作对。
datapath_en: Phantom records human demonstrations with a single fixed RGBD camera placed at the robot's future viewpoint, then estimates the demonstrator's hand pose to derive gripper actions. It edits every frame by segmenting and inpainting out the human arm and overlaying a rendered robot arm, closing the visual embodiment gap so human video looks like robot video. The resulting imitation policy deploys zero-shot on real hardware. Phantom releases code and trained policies, not a language-annotation corpus.
datapath_cn: Phantom 使用放置在机器人未来视角处的单台固定 RGBD 相机记录人类演示，并估计演示者的手部姿态以推导夹爪动作。它对每一帧进行编辑：分割并修复去除人手手臂，再叠加渲染的机器人手臂，从而弥合视觉本体差异，使人类视频看起来像机器人视频。最终得到的模仿策略可在真实硬件上零样本部署。Phantom 发布代码与训练好的策略，而非语言标注语料。
flow_en: inpaint human arm and overlay robot
flow_cn: 机器人视角下的人类视频
手部姿态估计得到夹爪动作
修复去除人手并叠加机器人
no_corpus_en: Phantom is a human-to-robot imitation method, not an annotation source. Its supervision comes from extracted hand poses and edited frames rather than text, and it releases code (MIT) and policies, not a new language corpus. This atlas documents Phantom descriptively rather than clustering downloaded strings.
no_corpus_cn: Phantom 是一种人到机器人的模仿方法，而非标注来源。它的监督来自提取的手部姿态与编辑后的图像，而非文本，并以 MIT 许可发布代码与策略，而非新的语言语料。因此本图谱以描述性方式记录 Phantom，而非对下载的字符串进行聚类。
example_caption_en: Phantom trains policies from human-only video recorded at the robot's viewpoint; the project page shows demonstration and rollout clips.
example_caption_cn: Phantom 仅用机器人视角拍摄的人类视频训练策略；项目主页提供演示与执行片段。
provenance: Verified via arXiv 2503.00779, github.com/MarionLepert/phantom (MIT), and phantom-human-videos.github.io. The camera is fixed at the robot viewpoint, not head-mounted.
citation: Lepert, Fang, Bohg, Phantom: Training Robots Without Robots Using Only Human Videos, CoRL 2025 (arXiv:2503.00779).
